hypothesis: A tagline that combines emotional resonance with practical clarity will outperform purely aspirational or purely functional taglines. A culturally-rooted message (Hindi or Indian-pride framing) will outperform a generic English tagline for the Indian creative-tool market.
researchQuestion: Of five candidate marketing taglines for Adobe Express in India — three English (Magic of design. In your hands. / Now anyone can design / Empowering Indians to design) and two with Hindi or Hinglish framing (Ek click mein design / Ab India karega design) — which resonates most strongly with people who regularly use creative tools, drives the highest stated intent to try the app, and avoids the failure modes of vagueness, cliché, condescension or linguistic exclusion?
productDescription: Adobe Express, India — Adobe's consumer-facing creative design tool for people with light creative design needs (social posts, reels, edited images, flyers, posters). India is an explicit growth market and the tagline is one of the core levers for the localised positioning of the app. The five taglines are presented to participants as standalone TEXT only — no Adobe brand attribution and no visuals — to isolate the message itself from the lift that the Adobe brand would otherwise provide. Findings from this round are directional input into a planned second high-fidelity round where taglines will be paired with the Adobe Express name and product visuals.
targetAudience: People in India who regularly use creative tools for light design needs. The base skews younger (mean age ~31.6, median 25-34 yrs; 18-44 makes up ~88%), is multilingual (English 100%, Hindi 89%, Marathi 22%), and is template-driven in its workflow (top tools: Canva 92%, Adobe Photoshop 55%, CapCut 49%, Adobe Express 25%). Four overlapping cohorts: (1) Massy Consumer / Personal — salaried employees, homemakers, non-employed individuals making designs for personal use; (2) Small Business / Solopreneurs — freelancers, gig workers and small business owners using design for client or commercial work; (3) Students — full-time students some of whom freelance on the side; (4) Creators / Influencers — a mostly-overlapping pool of monetising or aspiring creators including photographers, content writers and graphic designers. Primary content created: short-form Videos / Reels (75%), Social media posts (73%), Edited images (67%).
objectives:
1. Quantify each of the five taglines on a 1-5 resonance scale and rank them by average rating.
2. Identify which tagline drives the highest stated intent-to-try-the-app via a single forced-choice question with open-ended reasoning.
3. Surface the top three positive and top three negative sentiment themes per tagline with representative quotes.
4. Distil cross-tagline strategic insights — the tensions between aspiration and functional clarity, the appeal of speed and simplicity, and the double-edged sword of national-identity messaging.
5. Land a single, defended primary tagline recommendation with an explicit caveat about the absence of brand context and the need for a follow-up high-fidelity round.
Intended deliverable: a scaled-qualitative findings report (n≈64) suitable for marcomms tagline selection for Adobe Express India.

variants.description: Magic of design. In your hands.
Ek click mein design (Design with a single click)
Ab India karega design (Now India will design)
